generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T PHALLUS. Phallus impudicus, L. Natural order , Fungi. Common name , Stinkhorn. Preparation , Tincture of whole fungus. Authority. Kaleniczensko, effects of an infusion, five or six spoonfuls, taken during twenty-four hours, for experiment, L'Union Méd., 1865, No. 34. HEAD. ► Vertigo. EYE. ► Difficult vision. ► Obscuration of vision. ► Objects seem colored gray, as in smoke. THROAT. ► Great dryness of the throat, with irritation, as from black pepper. STOMACH. ► Loss of appetite. ► Devouring thirst. ► Violent vomiting, with profuse salivation. ► Gastroenteralgia. ABDOMEN. [10.] ► Painful sensitiveness of the whole abdomen. STOOL. ► Watery diarrha. URINARY ORGANS. ► Urine deep-red, depositing a sediment of urates of lime and soda. RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Dry, oppressive cough. GENERALITIES. ► Great feebleness of the whole body. SLEEP. ► Sleepiness. FEVER. ► After a few days all the pores became opened; profuse viscid perspiration. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
